helpful: Per-fee-class grant rates in statpack.json (paid vs IFP) — the single most useful anchor for a paid petition
distribution_count on corpus query rows made the relist signal easy to check against recent grants
Provisioned petition text (QPs + reasons for granting) meant no fetching of the petition PDF was needed
MCP opinion fetch of an intervening SCOTUS QI decision (Zorn) took two calls and materially shaped the adjustment
gaps: The statpack.md in this checkout has no 'Segment base rate by salience band' table although the prompt describes one; anchored on the paid-class per-Term rate instead
No cheap way to ask 'how often does the Court grant/summarily reverse petitions where the respondent waived and no CFR issued' — a waiver/CFR cut in the statpack would sharpen petitions like this one
A topic/issue-area filter on SCOTUS corpus rows (e.g. qualified immunity) would let priors be matched by subject rather than only court/era/disposition
notes: Corpus query over the service backend reported a 40 MB ranged read for one 8-row query; fine within budget but worth knowing when planning multiple queries.
